(born 1973) is an English male [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").

["Bowls England men's senior international team"](https://web.archive.org/web/20180117012215/http://www.bowlsinternational.com/2017/09/26/bowls-england-mens-senior-international-team/). Bowls International. Archived from [the original](http://www.bowlsinternational.com/2017/09/26/bowls-england-mens-senior-international-team/) on 17 January 2018. Retrieved 16 January 2018.

["Past Records"](https://web.archive.org/web/20190327221853/https://www.bowlsengland.com/about-us/past-records). Bowls England. Archived from [the original](https://www.bowlsengland.com/about-us/past-records) on 27 March 2019. Retrieved 16 January 2018.
["HK Classic winners Men Singles"](http://www.hklba.org/new/index.php?option=com_content&view=article&id=144&Itemid=218&lang=en). HKLBA. Retrieved 7 June 2021.

["Bowls: City star David Bolt lands deserved Commonwealth Games call-up"](https://www.sunderlandecho.com/sport/more-sport/bowls-city-star-david-bolt-lands-deserved-commonwealth-games-call-up-1-8864113). Sunderland Echo.

["Fours results"](https://web.archive.org/web/20180413190409/https://results.gc2018.com/en/lawn-bowls/event-schedule-men-s-fours.htm). CG2018. Archived from [the original](https://results.gc2018.com/en/lawn-bowls/event-schedule-men-s-fours.htm) on 13 April 2018. Retrieved 13 April 2018.
["Commonwealth Games: Scotland's Alex Marshall wins record fifth gold as women take bronze in bowls"](https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/commonwealth-games/43748412). BBC Sport.

["Bowls England National Finals 2021"](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/1/d/e/2PACX-1vTES6_wJnq77Qpg4L82G5Aisntif5ROsAiyQgZquY9qfYSvZwO3VOo4AYnRLfSb8iwvYOAHDd49Yuwx/pubhtml#). Bowls England. Retrieved 22 August 2021.

["Bowls star hoping business venture will be just champion"](https://www.thenorthernecho.co.uk/business/14423772.bowls-star-hoping-business-venture-will-just-champion/). The Northern Echo. Retrieved 22 August 2021.

English bowls player (born 1973)

David Bolt

**David Bolt** (born 1973) is an English male [lawn and indoor bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls").

## Bowls career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_Bolt_(bowls)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bowls career")]

Bolt is an England international[[1]](#cite_note-1) and was the [National singles champion](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Singles_Four_Wood) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Singles Four Wood)") in 2011 and [National fours champion](/wiki/Bowls_England_National_Championships_(Men%27s_Fours) "Bowls England National Championships (Men's Fours)") in 2014 during the [Men's National Championships](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_Men%27s_National_Championships "List of Bowls England Men's National Championships").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 2016, he won the [Hong Kong International Bowls Classic](/wiki/Hong_Kong_International_Bowls_Classic "Hong Kong International Bowls Classic") singles title and the following year finished runner-up in the pairs with [Taylor Monk](/w/index.php?title=Taylor_Monk&action=edit&redlink=1 "Taylor Monk (page does not exist)").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

He was selected as part of the [English team](/wiki/England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "England at the 2018 Commonwealth Games") for the [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games") on the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland") in [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland")[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) where he claimed a [bronze medal](/wiki/Bronze_medal "Bronze medal") in the [Fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Men%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2018 Commonwealth Games – Men's fours") with [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney"), [Louis Ridout](/wiki/Louis_Ridout "Louis Ridout") and [Sam Tolchard](/wiki/Sam_Tolchard "Sam Tolchard").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In 2019 he won the triples bronze medal at the [Atlantic Bowls Championships](/wiki/Atlantic_Bowls_Championships "Atlantic Bowls Championships")[[8]](#cite_note-8) and in 2021 was runner-up to Sam Tolchard in the National singles.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=David_Bolt_(bowls)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Bolt was a postman by trade but set up a bowls shop business at the South Shields Bowling Centre in 2016.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
